item.title: Philosophy
item.description:
What I dislike

C++

C++ is a TERRIBLE language which has 10000 different ways to do something that should be easy, simple and elegant, while C has only one way. Everything is convoluted and complicated.

Python

Python like C++, the same ugly language. The scope even depends on indentation. Doing everything is so easy that you can’t feel the joy of coding.

Electron applications

It’s so heavy. It could have just run in the browser, but instead I have to install an entire extra browser just to run a single program. As the numbers of Electron apps increases, the proprietary browser on my computer also grows. Why is my storage space being used to install browsers? And it’s so heavy that it doesn’t run well on my ThinkPad X220.

item.title: Uses
item.description:
For more details, see uses, a list of /uses pages detailing developer setups, gear, software and configs.

Check out this page to see how I use electronic devices.

Hardware

Desktop

  • CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-10600KF (12) @ 4.80 GHz
  • GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX2060
  • Memory: 16GB
  • Disk: 1TB + 120GB
  • OS: Debian GNU/Linux 12 & Windows 11

ThinkPad X220

  • CPU: Intel i5 2450M
  • Memory: 8GB
  • Disk: 120GB
  • OS: Arch Linux

Phone and Tablet

I use an iQOO Neo7 SE that costs less than 1,000 RMB. On my phone, I basically only need to send and receive messages, make and receive calls, and use navigation. So buying this device was probably a loss. If WeChat were no longer used across China, I might be able to cut a few hundred RMB more and buy a second-hand device from seven or eight years ago.
